commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stable hashing, empty env var handling, SSE token extraction

- Strip volatile Claude Code tags (system-reminder, local-command-caveat,
  available-deferred-tools, etc.) before hashing messages so content
  matching survives between requests
- Use `or` pattern for env vars so empty strings fall back to defaults
  (fixes broken compressor URL when ROLLING_CONTEXT_SUMMARIZER_URL="")
- Remove empty-string defaults from settings.json hooks
- Extract input_tokens from both message_start and message_delta SSE
  events (CodeGate puts final tokens in message_delta)
- Char-based fallback when SSE has no usage data at all

commit.message:
Classify BinaryModulePathEq → §30.4.4.1 [skip ci]

The test exercises a state-dependent path with `if (a == b)`, where `==` is a `binary_module_path_operator` used as the conditional expression. §30.4.4.1 is the normative subclause that defines the conditional expression for state-dependent paths, lists valid operators (including `==`) in Table 30-1, and specifies their evaluation semantics. The grammar production itself is in Annex A.8.6, but the normative clause is §30.4.4.1.

commit.message:
fix: deduplicate choice edges and topological child selection in compute_choice_edges (#1185 #1187 #1188) (#1189)

## Summary

- **#1185**: `compute_choice_edges()` emitted duplicate `(from_passage,
to_passage)` pairs when multiple beats in the same intersection passage
independently diverged to the same target. Fixed by replacing
`list[ChoiceSpec]` accumulation with a `dict[(from, to), ChoiceSpec]`
that merges `grants` (set union) on collision.
- **#1187**: `sorted(path_children)[0]` (alphabetical) skipped gap-beat
passages when interleave added transitive edges making both a gap beat
and its successor direct children of a divergence beat. Fixed by
`_topo_first()` which picks the topologically earliest child using the
existing `children` adjacency dict.
- **#1188**: Test coverage gap — added
`TestChoiceEdgesIntersectionMultiBeat` (2 tests) and
`TestChoiceEdgesGapBeatChild` (1 test) modeling real GROW pipeline
output shapes.

## Design Conformance

Reviewed by `architect-reviewer` against
`docs/design/procedures/polish.md` Phase 4c and
`docs/design/document-3-ontology.md`.

| Requirement | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Find divergence beats (successors on different paths) | polish.md §4c
step 1 | CONFORMANT |
| Map divergence successors to passages | polish.md §4c step 2 |
CONFORMANT |
| ChoiceSpec: `from_passage` | polish.md §4c step 3 | CONFORMANT |
| ChoiceSpec: `to_passage` | polish.md §4c step 3 | CONFORMANT |
| ChoiceSpec: `requires` (state flags for gates) | polish.md §4c step 3
| CONFORMANT |
| ChoiceSpec: `grants` (state flags activated by choice) | polish.md §4c
step 3 | CONFORMANT |
| Labels deferred to Phase 5 | polish.md §4c step 4 | CONFORMANT |
| `requires` empty for most choices, gates after convergence | doc-3,
Part 5 | CONFORMANT |
| One choice per (from_passage, to_passage) pair | doc-3, Part 5 edge
table | CONFORMANT |
| Deduplicate choice edges (#1185) | Issue #1185 | CONFORMANT |
| Topological target beat selection (#1187) | Issue #1187 | CONFORMANT |
| `passage_id_to_spec` hoisted outside inner loop (#1188) | Issue #1188
| CONFORMANT |

0 MISSING / 0 DEAD findings.

## Test plan

- [ ] `uv run pytest
tests/unit/test_polish_deterministic.py::TestChoiceEdgesIntersectionMultiBeat
-x -q` — 2 tests pass
- [ ] `uv run pytest
tests/unit/test_polish_deterministic.py::TestChoiceEdgesGapBeatChild -x
-q` — 1 test passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_polish_deterministic.py -x -q` —
all existing tests continue to pass

Closes #1185
Closes #1187
Closes #1188

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add workspace creation and actor account creation

Users can now create their account directly from the login screen.
On account creation, a personal workspace is automatically provisioned.
Workspace owners can invite collaborators, who share the same note view.

Key changes:
- Add Workspace entity with owner + member tracking
- Add createActor() to ActorDataSource / repository / notifier
- Redesign LoginPage: existing actors selectable + "Create account" form
  with name input and colour picker
- Auto-create workspace on first login (actor creation flow)
- HomePage shows workspace name in the title bar
- Invite collaborator button on HomePage → creates actor + joins workspace
- Workspace-aware note filtering: members see notes from all workspace members
- New DB migration: workspaces + workspace_members tables
- Update and extend tests (workspace entity, repository, login page, home page)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* feat: auto-create account on first launch (no login screen)

On first launch the app now silently provisions an account and workspace
for the user — no form to fill in, no login screen to navigate past.

- DeviceSessionService: stores the actor ID in SharedPreferences so the
  correct actor is restored on every subsequent launch.
- SplashWrapper rewritten as ConsumerStatefulWidget:
  · Runs session init + 2-second splash in parallel (Future.wait).
  · Known device  → restores saved actor and loads their workspace.
  · First launch  → dev: auto-selects first seeded actor ("Me");
                    prod: creates actor ("My Account") + workspace.
  · Actor deleted → auto-creates a fresh actor (DB reset resilience).
- Removed the LoginPage from the main navigation flow; login picker is
  still accessible via the avatar button in the home screen app bar.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: resolve flutter analyze warnings

- Remove erroneous await on void _loginAs() in login_page.dart
- Remove redundant null assertion on already-guarded _currentActorId in checklist_providers.dart

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: scroll Cancel button into view before tapping in login_page_test

The default 800x600 test viewport clips the Cancel button when actor tiles
and the create-account form together exceed the screen height. Adding
ensureVisible() scrolls it into view so the hit-test succeeds.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* feat: add in-app logging with AppLogger and LogViewerPage

- AppLogger: in-memory singleton that stores LogEntry list and prints
  via debugPrint; clear() and log() both notify LogNotifier
- LogNotifier: ChangeNotifier wired into AppLogger so UI widgets
  update reactively when new entries arrive
- LogViewerPage: reverse-chronological log list with error styling,
  copy-to-clipboard on long-press, and clear button
- Home page: dev-mode bug icon in app bar with Badge (red dot when
  errors present); tapping opens LogViewerPage
- All repository catch blocks upgraded to catch (e, s) with
  AppLogger.instance.log() and real exception in failure message

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* feat: make log viewer text selectable and add copy-all button

- Replace Text with SelectableText for timestamp, message, and error
  so users can tap-and-drag to select any portion of a log entry
- Add 'Copy all logs' icon button in app bar to copy every entry to
  clipboard in one tap (newest-first, formatted with timestamp prefix)
- Extract _formatTime as top-level function so app bar action can reuse it

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: make LogViewerPage reactive to new log entries

Subscribe to LogNotifier in initState/dispose so the page rebuilds
when AppLogger receives new entries — the copy-all and clear buttons
now appear as soon as the first entry is logged, even if the page
was already open.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: always show copy/clear buttons in LogViewerPage

Buttons were hidden behind if (entries.isNotEmpty) so they never
appeared on first open. Now always visible; onPressed is null
(greyed out) when empty, active when there are entries.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: show log viewer button in all environments

The bug icon was gated on AppConstants.isDev so it was invisible in
the prod APK. Users need access to logs in prod to diagnose real
errors, so remove the isDev guard.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: add INTERNET permission to AndroidManifest

Without this Android blocks all network calls at the OS level,
which manifests as DNS lookup failures for Supabase requests.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

* fix: widen color_value to BIGINT to handle full Flutter color range

Flutter ARGB colors are 32-bit unsigned ints; values above 0x7FFFFFFF
overflow PostgreSQL INTEGER. BIGINT handles the full range.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01DnBArNqWrtRaVyAjuNUxWh

---------

commit.message:
fix(ci): use GITHUB_WORKFLOW_REF for keyless verify-blob identity

The hardcoded --certificate-identity referenced the wrong org
(x0tta6bl4 vs x0tta6bl4-ai) and wrong workflow file (ci.yml vs
ebpf-release-signing.yml). Use GITHUB_WORKFLOW_REF which GitHub
Actions sets automatically to the exact subject embedded in the
keyless OIDC certificate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(approach): align ILS approach path with runway centerline

Previous approach waypoints created a curved/diagonal path (lat changed
from 37.48 to 37.49). Now all waypoints are at lat 37.49, matching
runway 28L centerline for a realistic straight-in ILS approach.

Approach path (from east):
- FAF at 12 NM, 6000 ft
- Intermediate at 8 NM, 4000 ft
- 4 NM point at 2000 ft
- Short final at 1 NM, 500 ft
- Threshold at 50 ft

Also fixed departure path to climb on runway heading before turning.

commit.message:
feat(parser): multi-bank extraction for all 5 SA banks

Add bank-specific parsing in regex strategy for FNB (Cr suffix),
ABSA (R-prefix and space-separated thousands), Capitec (dual dates),
and Nedbank (transaction number prefix). Add balance-delta sign
inference for banks without explicit column position data.

Results across 11 real PDFs:
- Standard Bank: 100% balance accuracy (699 txns)
- FNB: 90-96% accuracy (473 txns)
- ABSA: 83-91% accuracy (99 txns)
- Nedbank: 86-88% accuracy (81 txns)
- Capitec: 14-60% accuracy (278 txns, needs improvement)

commit.message:
Fix assembly comment syntax: replace C-style // with GAS-compatible # in userland .S files

The GNU assembler (GAS) for x86_64 does not support // as inline comments
after instructions. This caused CI build failures on netstat.S and would
affect other files with newer assembler versions.

Fix unresponsive buttons in card view — swipe was stealing taps

Three fixes:
1. Swipe handler now ignores touches that start on buttons/inputs,
   so taps on +/-, GIR, FWY, notes always register
2. Increased swipe thresholds (50px move, 100px to trigger nav)
   to prevent accidental swipes
3. Added touch-action:manipulation to all interactive elements
   to eliminate 300ms tap delay on mobile

Also enlarged +/- buttons from 42px to 52px for easier tapping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: correct import guide to match actual implementation

The import documentation was showing multiple formats (CSV, S7, Modbus, OPC UA)
that don't match the actual implementation. The real code only supports one
simple PLC-style declaration format:

    Alias : DataType AT Address;

Changes:
- Remove fictional format selection (no CoDeSys/S7/Modbus formats)
- Remove CSV import examples (not implemented)
- Correct syntax to match parseTagLine() regex
- Add real API usage examples
- Update supported data types (BOOL, INT, UINT, DINT, UDINT, REAL, STRING, WORD)
- Remove references to non-existent UI features

The actual implementation in tags_import_export.go:
- Parses: ^(\S+)\s*:\s*(\w+)\s+AT\s+(.+)$
- Supports // comments
- Ignores empty lines
- Semicolon is optional
- Address stored as-is in database

commit.message:
refactor: upgrade to Python 3.14, adopt PEP 758 syntax, and reorganize documentation (#31)

## Summary

Comprehensive refactoring to modernize the project with Python 3.14,
improve documentation organization, and adopt new language features.
Part of upcoming major release.

## ⚠️ BREAKING CHANGES

**Python 3.14+ Required** (was 3.11+)
- Dropped support for Python 3.11, 3.12, 3.13
- Adopted PEP 758 bracketless exception syntax: `except A, B:` instead
of `except (A, B):`
- CI now tests only Python 3.14
- Projects requiring older Python must pin to current stable release

## Key Changes

### 1. **Documentation Refactoring** 📚

**New Documents:**
- **SPEC.md** (8K): Comprehensive architecture & design specification
- **CONTRIBUTING.md** (5.5K): Complete development workflow guide  
- **CHANGELOG.md**: Version history and release notes

**Refactored Existing:**
- **CLAUDE.md** (430→71 lines): Lean 1-page quick reference with
navigation + tool priority strategy
- **README.md** (242→171 lines): Eliminated duplication, user-focused
- **TODO.md**: Reorganized by priority (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) with effort
estimates

**Code Documentation:**
- Module-level docstrings: track.py, sqlite.py, spotify/misc.py
- Enhanced testing patterns documentation in conftest.py

### 2. **Python 3.14 Modernization** ⬆️

- Adopted PEP 758 bracketless exception syntax
- Updated all version references across documentation
- Updated CI matrix to test only Python 3.14
- Updated Makefile test-all-versions target

### 3. **Permissions & Security Optimization** 🔒

- Consolidated git/gh subcommands into `Bash(git:*)` and `Bash(gh:*)`
- Added deny list to prevent dangerous operations (--force, --hard,
etc.)
- Removed tool duplicates: cat, grep, jq, chmod, ls, pwd, mkdir, tree
- Reduced permissions from 45 → 20 (57% reduction)

### 4. **Development Best Practices** ✨

- Added tool priority strategy (use Read/Grep/Glob/Edit/Write before
bash)
- Added commit strategy: only commit files changed by agents
- Enhanced testing patterns documentation
- Cleaned up temporary analysis artifacts

### 5. **Project Housekeeping** 🧹

- Updated author email to GitHub noreply address
- Fixed broken documentation links
- Clarified CI/CD configuration comments

## Files Changed

**Documentation (7 files):**
- README.md, SPEC.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md, TODO.md, CLAUDE.md,
.github/workflows/tests.yml

**Configuration (2 files):**
- pyproject.toml (requires-python: >=3.11 → >=3.14)
- .claude/settings.json (45 → 20 permissions, added deny list)

**Code & Tests (4 files):**
- spotfm/track.py, spotfm/sqlite.py, spotfm/spotify/misc.py,
tests/conftest.py

**Build (1 file):**
- Makefile

## Testing

✅ All 277 tests passing  
✅ 79% coverage maintained  
✅ Pre-commit hooks: 9/9 passing  
✅ No regressions introduced

## Impact

**Users**: Complete reference documentation, clear setup path,
comprehensive feature guide

**Contributors**: Detailed development guide, clear navigation, reduced
onboarding friction

**Agents**: Tool priority strategy, commit best practices, comprehensive
architecture docs

reduction

commit.message:
迭代第112轮补充: 修复布局dynamic import兼容性+优惠券/收藏UX增强

- 修复ShopLayout中ssr:false不兼容Server Component的问题
- 优惠券页面/收藏页面UX增强(auto-hooks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix feed stuck on skeleton when social loaders hang

Social data loaders (profile, following, friend requests) had no timeout.
If any Supabase query hung on slow network, Promise.all never resolved and
loadFeed() never ran, leaving skeleton placeholders indefinitely.

Added 8-second safety net that fires loadFeed() regardless, with a guard
to prevent double-execution when social data loads normally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: wristlog
repository.description: Watch tracker

commit.message:
fix: increase MAX_TOKENS to 8192 to prevent report truncation

Portfolio-level queries covering 6 deals with per-deal factor
scorecard tables were being truncated at 4096 tokens. The Big 4
voice prompt enforces brevity per point; the token limit should
not cut off comprehensive analysis mid-table.

Also: update wrap-up nudge and synthesis prompts to encourage
complete portfolio coverage rather than artificial word limits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: enhance session stats and add completion status to session log

Settings sheet stats now show Focus Minutes Today, Total Sessions,
Completion Rate, and Total Courses (replacing just courses + music).
Session log table now includes a Status column with Done/Partial badges
and shows planned vs actual duration when they differ.
